objective: Resolve the highest-value residual issues from the Phase5 parallel run investigation while preserving the current strict default scheduler behavior.
scope: Parallel-step launch policy for cautious task lanes
Operator visibility in show/lane summary for blocked or rejected lane candidates
Rejected Phase6 task-state recovery and display consistency
Focused regression coverage for scheduler, package, recovery, and UI summary behavior
Investigation memo status updates after implementation
out_of_scope: Directly rewriting existing run-state files under runs/
Changing the sample app product files under examples/parallel_smoke_system/
Making cautious lanes launch by default
Broad redesign of task grouping semantics
Full task contract lint/preflight for every possible file reference pattern unless it is needed by the implemented slice
constraints: Preserve unrelated user changes in tasks/, outputs/, docs/worklog/, docs/investigations/, and examples/parallel_smoke_system/.
Use normal relay-dev entrypoints and tests; do not manually patch canonical run-state to force progress.
Workers are not alone in the codebase and must not revert or overwrite edits outside their assigned write scope.
Parent agent owns final integration, full regression, and final reporting.
assumptions: The strict default should continue to reject non-parallel lanes unless an explicit opt-in is supplied.
A cautious opt-in should allow cautious lanes but should not accidentally allow serial lanes.
Operator visibility can be improved without changing scheduler eligibility semantics.
The current run inconsistency is best handled by code-level repair/display improvements, not direct mutation of the run files.
Non-task-scoped rollback targets such as Phase4 are broader planning rollback decisions and remain out of scope for automatic task-lane repair in this slice.

tasks.title: Add cautious lane opt-in
tasks.intent: Let operators explicitly launch cautious lanes while keeping default parallel-step strict.
acceptance: CLI exposes an explicit AllowCautious-style switch for parallel-step.
Default parallel-step still rejects cautious and serial candidates.
The new opt-in allows cautious candidates only; serial candidates remain rejected unless an existing broader non-parallel test path explicitly allows them.
Rejected candidate errors distinguish cautious from serial where practical.
risks: Existing internal AllowNonParallelSafety behavior may be shared by group-step tests and must not be broken.
CLI parameter naming must remain compatible with existing scripts.

tasks.title: Explain lane blocking in show
tasks.intent: Make running-but-idle and ready-but-not-launchable states understandable from show output.
acceptance: lane_summary waiting tasks include a concrete reason for cautious, serial, dependency, active job, and non task-scoped current phase cases when detectable.
lane_summary exposes additive blocking detail that aligns with the cautious opt-in introduced by IRD-01, dependency repair, or normal waiting.
Existing summary fields remain backward compatible.
risks: Show output is consumed as JSON, so new fields should be additive.
Do not make the UI summary depend on package creation side effects.

tasks.title: Clarify rejected Phase6 recovery limits
tasks.intent: Strengthen coverage for supported task-scoped Phase6 reject recovery and explicitly preserve non-task rollback as a remaining design issue.
acceptance: Repair-RejectedPhase6TaskState continues to reopen stale rejected Phase6 lanes to a valid task-scoped rollback_phase such as Phase5.
Tests cover a task with last_completed_phase Phase6, status in_progress, phase_cursor Phase6, and canonical reject artifact rollback_phase Phase5.
Tests make clear that non-task rollback_phase values such as Phase4 are not automatically task-lane repaired in this slice.
risks: Rollback to Phase4 is not task-scoped in existing helpers and must remain documented as a residual design issue.
A repair must not override active sibling jobs or pending approvals.

tasks.title: Update investigation memo
tasks.intent: Keep docs/investigations accurate after implementation and verification.
acceptance: Implemented items move from 未対応 to 対応済み or 一部緩和済み with test evidence.
Remaining issues explicitly include any intentionally deferred task contract lint/preflight work.
The current-run description no longer overstates rollback if run-state and artifact state remain different.
risks: Docs must not claim that sample run files were mutated if only code/tests changed.
